description: Operational runbooks for common failure modes.

runbooks.title: High latency / elevated p99
symptoms: Slow page loads / timeouts at edge
Latency rising without proportional error-rate change
investigation_steps: Confirm scope in metrics.json: which service(s), is latency the leading signal vs CPU/memory?
Check recent deployments.json for the affected service in the hours before onset.
Inspect logs.json for the service: GC pauses, cache timeouts, downstream call latency, pool waits.
Walk the dependency graph (service_dependencies.json) downstream to find the slowest hop.
Check cache hit-rate and DB connection wait — latency often shifts from cache to DB.
mitigation_steps: If correlated with a deploy, roll back.
Scale out the bottleneck service; raise downstream timeouts/circuit-breaker thresholds.
Shed non-critical load; enable caching / increase TTL if cache-related.
runbooks.escalation: If customer-facing > 15m or revenue path affected, page service owner and declare SEV2; pull in Platform for shared infra.

runbooks.title: Database connection exhaustion
symptoms: 'connection is not available' / 'timeout waiting for connection from pool'
PostgreSQL 'too many clients already' / 'remaining connection slots reserved'
Error-rate spike with latency spike; healthy DB CPU but maxed connections
investigation_steps: Grep logs.json for pool/connection timeout messages and the owning service.
Compare onset against deployments.json — did a deploy change pool sizing or datasource config?
Check pool_size * replica_count against the database max_connections.
Look for long-running / blocked queries holding connections.
mitigation_steps: Roll back the offending deploy if pool config changed.
Right-size pool (pool_size * replicas < max_connections); restart pods to reset pools.
Introduce / front with PgBouncer; add statement + pool acquisition timeouts.
runbooks.escalation: Page DB on-call (Platform) if max_connections must be raised or replicas added; SEV2 if checkout/payment affected.

runbooks.title: Payment failures
symptoms: Authorization error-rate spike; checkout success-rate drop
Provider 5xx/timeouts, UnknownHostException, or pool/connection errors inside payment-service
investigation_steps: Distinguish external vs internal: provider 5xx/timeout (external) vs pool/DNS/config errors (internal).
Check deployments.json for a recent payment-service change (datasource, pool, client config).
Grep logs.json for provider host errors, DNS (UnknownHostException), and HikariPool timeouts.
Correlate error-rate in metrics.json with deploy time and DNS-lookup latency.
mitigation_steps: If deploy-correlated, roll back payment-service.
If provider-side, fail over provider region / enable retry queue and circuit breaker.
If DNS, flush/scale CoreDNS, enable NodeLocal DNSCache, pre-resolve provider host.
runbooks.escalation: SEV1 if authorization broadly failing; page Payments on-call and notify Finance/Fraud.

runbooks.title: Service crash loop / pod restarts
symptoms: Memory sawtooth in metrics; 'OutOfMemoryError' or liveness-probe failures in logs
investigation_steps: Check restart cause: OOMKilled vs liveness failure vs panic (logs.json around restart time).
In metrics.json, look for memory climbing to the limit before each restart (leak) vs flat (config).
Correlate with deployments.json — did a deploy change heap/limits or introduce a cache/listener?
Capture a heap dump before the next restart for retention analysis.
mitigation_steps: Rolling restart for immediate relief; raise memory limit as a stopgap only.
Roll back the deploy that changed memory behavior.
Bound caches / fix listener accumulation; add eviction.
runbooks.escalation: SEV2 if restarts affect a critical service's capacity; page owning team.

runbooks.title: Kafka consumer lag
symptoms: Growing consumer lag; stale data downstream (e.g. stock counts), delayed events
Frequent consumer group rebalances; processing throughput < produce rate
investigation_steps: Quantify lag per topic/partition over time in logs.json/metrics.json.
Identify the slow step in the consumer (DB writes, external calls, GC).
Check for rebalance storms (consumer restarts, session timeouts).
Assess downstream impact: in inventory's case, can lag permit overselling?
mitigation_steps: Scale the consumer group (<= partition count); add partitions if at ceiling.
Batch DB writes; tune max.poll.records / max.poll.interval.ms; make processing idempotent.
Temporarily gate risky actions (e.g. block sales of low-stock SKUs) until lag drains.
runbooks.escalation: SEV2 if lag risks data integrity (overselling / double-charge); page Fulfilment + Platform (Kafka).

runbooks.title: Memory leak
symptoms: Heap-after-GC trends upward over hours/days; GC pause time grows; periodic OOMKill
Memory sawtooth correlated with restarts in metrics
investigation_steps: Confirm leak vs load: heap-after-GC rising independent of traffic = leak.
Pin the start to a deployment (new cache, listener, library) in deployments.json.
Take heap dumps at two points; diff dominator tree for growing retained sets.
mitigation_steps: Rolling restarts on a schedule as a stopgap.
Roll back / patch the introducing change; bound the offending structure.
runbooks.escalation: SEV2 if capacity erodes faster than restarts can recover; involve owning team + Platform.

runbooks.title: DNS resolution failures
symptoms: UnknownHostException / 'name or service not known'; intermittent and self-clearing
Elevated DNS lookup latency; errors not correlated with any provider incident
investigation_steps: Check whether failures are intermittent and clear on retry (DNS signature) vs sustained (provider).
Grep logs.json for UnknownHostException and DNS lookup latency warnings; note the hostname.
Check CoreDNS / NodeLocal DNSCache health and query latency; look for ndots-amplified lookups.
Confirm the target provider has no concurrent incident (rules out provider-side).
mitigation_steps: Scale CoreDNS; enable NodeLocal DNSCache; raise cache TTL.
Add pod dnsConfig (reduce ndots); pre-resolve / pin critical external hosts.
Add retry-with-backoff around resolution for critical egress.
runbooks.escalation: Page Platform (networking) if cluster-wide; SEV2 if it disrupts payment/critical egress.

runbooks.title: Cache failures / Redis issues
symptoms: JedisConnectionException / 'could not get a resource from the pool' / cache GET timeouts
Latency spike as traffic falls back to DB; cache hit-rate collapse; possible stampede
investigation_steps: Identify the affected Redis cluster and owning service from logs.json.
Check for ElastiCache failover events and client reconnect storms.
Distinguish stampede (mass TTL expiry -> DB spike) from connectivity (pool exhaustion / timeouts).
Correlate cache-error onset with latency in metrics.json and any client-library deploy.
mitigation_steps: Tune client reconnect + pool size; add cache-miss timeouts and bulkheads.
For stampede: TTL jitter, single-flight, pre-warm hot keys.
Degrade gracefully to DB with strict timeouts; circuit-break the cache if unhealthy.
runbooks.escalation: SEV2 if checkout latency breaches SLO; page owning team + Platform (ElastiCache).
